sanskrit: ते तं भुक्त्वा स्वर्गलोकं विशालं क्षीणे पुण्ये मर्त्यलोकं विशन्ति । एवं त्रयीधर्ममनुप्रपन्ना गतागतं कामकामा लभन्ते ॥
transliteration: te taṃ bhuktvā svarga-lokaṃ viśālaṃ kṣīṇe puṇye martya-lokaṃ viśanti | evaṃ trayī-dharmam anuprapannā gatāgataṃ kāma-kāmā labhante
literal_meaning: They, having enjoyed the vast heavenly world, when merit is exhausted enter the mortal world; thus, following the three-Veda dharma, desiring desires, they obtain the cycle of coming and going.
contextual_meaning: Sensory rewards—however refined—are finite; clinging to them perpetuates the anxiety of impermanence and the dread of inevitable loss.
psychological_state: Post-reward crash; the moment after the high when the mind intuits the next low.
trigger_scenario: The day after a promotion or big purchase when the novelty fades and the inbox refills.
anchor_text: Enjoy life’s pleasures, but hold them lightly; only the eternal frees you from return.
